channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: European Union ( EU ) Youth Conference 2026: Call for Group Facilitators & Group Harvesters (Paid Roles) — Ireland’s EU Presidency
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
Between 1 July and 31 December 2026, Ireland will hold the Presidency of the Council of the European Union. As part of this historic period, the National Youth Council of Ireland (NYCI), in partnership with the Department of Education and Youth, is preparing to host one of the most significant youth‑policy events of the year: […]

The post European Union ( EU ) Youth Conference 2026: Call for Group Facilitators & Group Harvesters (Paid Roles) — Ireland’s EU Presidency appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Between 1 July and 31 December 2026, Ireland will hold the Presidency of the Council of the European Union. As part of this historic period, the National Youth Council of Ireland (NYCI), in partnership with the Department of Education and Youth, is preparing to host one of the most significant youth‑policy events of the year: the European Union Youth Conference (EUYC), taking place in Dublin from 27–30 September 2026.

To support the delivery of this major international gathering, NYCI is inviting applications for 10 Group Facilitators and 10 Group Harvesters. These are paid roles, with travel, accommodation, and meals fully covered. Both roles are essential to ensuring meaningful youth participation, high‑quality dialogue, and strong policy outcomes aligned with EU Youth Goal #4: Information and Constructive Dialogue.

Deadline to apply for both roles: Sunday, 7 June 2026 at 23:59 IST

Overview of the EU Youth Conference 2026

The EU Youth Conference brings together young people, policymakers, youth workers, and experts from across Europe to shape the direction of EU youth policy. During Ireland’s EU Presidency, the conference will focus on strengthening access to reliable information, improving dialogue between young people and decision‑makers, and advancing youth participation across Europe.

Participants will collaborate in structured workshops, discussions, and policy‑shaping sessions. The work of facilitators and harvesters will directly contribute to the EU Youth Dialogue process, influencing recommendations at the European level.

Call for Group Facilitators

NYCI is seeking 10 skilled Group Facilitators to lead and guide workshop groups throughout the conference.

Role Purpose

Facilitators will support young participants (18+), policymakers, and experts in exploring key issues related to Youth Goal #4. They will create inclusive spaces, encourage meaningful dialogue, and help groups develop clear, actionable recommendations.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead structured workshop sessions during the EUYC
  • Guide diverse groups through discussions and collaborative tasks
  • Ensure inclusive, respectful, and productive dialogue
  • Support participants in developing policy recommendations
  • Attend required online and in‑person preparatory meetings
  • Work on‑site in Dublin from 26–30 September 2026

Key Details

  • Fee: €350 per day (approx. 6 days including preparation)
  • Travel, accommodation, and meals provided
  • Mandatory participation in preparatory meetings
  • Work dates: Travel on 25 September; work from 26–30 September

Who Should Apply

Ideal candidates will have:

  • Strong workshop facilitation and group‑management skills
  • Experience working with diverse or international groups
  • Interest in youth participation, youth policy, and international cooperation

Further details are available in the official Facilitator Call document.

For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here

Call for Group Harvesters

NYCI is also recruiting 10 Group Harvesters to document discussions and capture outcomes during the conference.

Role Purpose

Harvesters play a crucial role in ensuring that ideas, insights, and recommendations from each workshop are accurately recorded. Their work feeds directly into the EU Youth Dialogue reporting process.

Key Responsibilities

  • Capture workshop discussions in real time
  • Summarize key points, recommendations, and outcomes
  • Support facilitators in documenting group progress
  • Prepare clear written outputs for EUYC reporting
  • Participate in online and in‑person preparatory meetings
  • Work on‑site in Dublin from 26–30 September 2026

Key Details

  • Fee: €300 per day (approx. 5 days including preparation)
  • Travel, accommodation, and meals provided
  • Required participation in preparatory meetings
  • Work dates: Travel on 25 September; work from 26–30 September

Who Should Apply

Ideal candidates will have:

  • Strong notetaking and reporting skills
  • Experience documenting workshops or group discussions
  • Interest in youth policy, youth participation, and international work

Further details are available in the official Harvester Call document.

Application Requirements

Applicants for both roles must confirm:

They are available to travel on Friday, 25 September 2026, and work in Dublin from Saturday 26 September to Wednesday 30 September 2026.

Applicants may also opt to join NYCI’s mailing list to receive updates about youth‑sector news and opportunities.

For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here

Deadline

Applications for both roles must be submitted by Sunday, 7 June 2026 at 23:59 IST.

 

Discover more  opportunities here

The post European Union ( EU ) Youth Conference 2026: Call for Group Facilitators & Group Harvesters (Paid Roles) — Ireland’s EU Presidency appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: National Youth Agency Youth Voice and Influence Consultants: Empowering Young Leaders to Shape Youth Advocacy in England
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The National Youth Agency (NYA), the Professional, Statutory and Regulatory Body (PSRB) for youth work in England, has announced an exciting consultancy opportunity for young people who are passionate about shaping national conversations and strengthening youth‑led advocacy. This initiative reflects NYA’s long‑standing commitment to elevating youth voice, empowering young leaders, and ensuring that young people […]

The post National Youth Agency Youth Voice and Influence Consultants: Empowering Young Leaders to Shape Youth Advocacy in England appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The National Youth Agency (NYA), the Professional, Statutory and Regulatory Body (PSRB) for youth work in England, has announced an exciting consultancy opportunity for young people who are passionate about shaping national conversations and strengthening youth‑led advocacy. This initiative reflects NYA’s long‑standing commitment to elevating youth voice, empowering young leaders, and ensuring that young people play an active role in influencing decisions that affect their lives and communities.

This article provides a comprehensive overview of the Youth Voice and Influence Consultant role, its purpose, responsibilities, benefits, and how young people can get involved.

About the Youth Voice and Influence Consultant Role

NYA is recruiting up to six young people to join its team as Youth Voice and Influence Consultants. These consultants will contribute directly to NYA’s mission by helping shape youth‑focused programmes, strengthening youth participation frameworks, and ensuring that young people’s perspectives are embedded across national youth sector initiatives.

This consultancy role is designed to be flexible, accessible, and supportive of young people’s diverse schedules, educational commitments, and personal circumstances.

Key Features of the Consultancy Opportunity

The Youth Voice and Influence Consultant role offers a unique blend of professional development, leadership experience, and national‑level influence. Key features include:

1. Flexible Working Hours

  • Consultants will work a minimum of 10 hours per month.
  • Hours are casual and flexible, allowing young people to balance the role with school, college, university, or employment.

2. Competitive Pay

  • The role offers £13.80 per hour, inclusive of rolled‑up holiday pay.
  • This ensures fair compensation for young people’s time, expertise, and lived experience.

3. Remote‑First Structure

  • The position is home‑based/remote, making it accessible to young people across England.
  • Consultants may occasionally travel to meetings, events, conferences, and youth sector gatherings.

4. Meaningful Youth‑Led Influence

Consultants will contribute to:

  • Strengthening youth voice in national programmes
  • Supporting youth‑led advocacy
  • Advising on youth engagement strategies
  • Shaping NYA’s work with policymakers, partners, and youth organisations
  • Ensuring young people’s lived experiences inform national decisions

Why This Opportunity Matters

Youth voice is a cornerstone of effective youth work. By recruiting young consultants, NYA ensures that:

  • Young people are not just consulted—they are actively involved in shaping decisions
  • Youth‑led insights influence national policy, programme design, and sector priorities
  • The youth sector remains responsive, inclusive, and grounded in real experiences
  • Young leaders gain professional experience, confidence, and national exposure

This consultancy role is more than a job—it is a platform for young people to drive meaningful change.

Who Should Consider Applying?

This opportunity is ideal for young people who:

  • Are passionate about youth voice, advocacy, and leadership
  • Want to influence national youth sector decisions
  • Have lived experience that can inform youth‑focused programmes
  • Are eager to develop professional skills in communication, leadership, and policy engagement
  • Are motivated to represent the perspectives of diverse young people across England

Deadline

Application deadline: June 7, 2026

For more information about this opportunity ad to apply visit here

Discover more job opportunities here

The post National Youth Agency Youth Voice and Influence Consultants: Empowering Young Leaders to Shape Youth Advocacy in England appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations ICAO Associate Administrative Officer (P2) Position in Montreal: Full Role Overview, Responsibilities, and Application Details
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The United Nations, through the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), has announced a new opportunity for early‑career professionals seeking to advance their administrative and management expertise within a global aviation governance environment. The Associate Administrative Officer (P2) role, based in Montreal, supports the Air Transport Bureau (ATB) in delivering ICAO’s strategic goals and strengthening administrative […]

The post United Nations ICAO Associate Administrative Officer (P2) Position in Montreal: Full Role Overview, Responsibilities, and Application Details appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations, through the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), has announced a new opportunity for early‑career professionals seeking to advance their administrative and management expertise within a global aviation governance environment. The Associate Administrative Officer (P2) role, based in Montreal, supports the Air Transport Bureau (ATB) in delivering ICAO’s strategic goals and strengthening administrative efficiency across the organization.

 

Deadline: 25 June 2026

About the Air Transport Bureau (ATB)

The Air Transport Bureau is central to ICAO’s mission of ensuring safe, secure, environmentally sustainable, and economically viable global aviation. It supports ICAO’s strategic goals, including:

  1. Every Flight Is Safe and Secure
  2. Aviation Is Environmentally Sustainable
  3. Aviation Delivers Seamless, Accessible, and Reliable Mobility for All
  4. Economic Development of Air Transport Ensures Prosperity and Societal Well‑Being
  5. No Country Left Behind

The Bureau provides expert assistance to multiple ICAO governing bodies, committees, and working groups, including:

  • Assembly
  • Council
  • Air Transport Committee (ATC)
  • Aviation Security Committee (ASC)
  • Ad Hoc Cybersecurity Coordination Committee (AHCCC)
  • Committee on Aviation Environmental Protection (CAEP)
  • Climate and Environment Committee (CEC)
  • Committee on Joint Support of Air Navigation Services (JSC)

It also supports specialized conferences, divisional meetings, and technical panels.

Key Functional Areas of the Bureau

The ATB is responsible for:

  • Developing and updating Standards and Recommended Practices (SARPs) in Annexes 9, 16, and 17
  • Formulating air transport policies and guidance
  • Supporting States in implementing CO₂ emissions reduction action plans
  • Managing the ICAO Traveller Identification Programme (TRIP) Strategy
  • Implementing the ICAO Global Aviation Security Plan (GASeP)
  • Conducting the Universal Security Audit Programme (USAP)
  • Maintaining global aviation data, statistics, and forecasts
  • Coordinating with UN bodies, international organizations, and regional aviation entities

Role Overview: Associate Administrative Officer (P2)

The Associate Administrative Officer plays a pivotal role in strengthening HR, administrative, and operational functions within the ATB. The position supports the development of a customer‑service‑oriented HR culture that emphasizes innovation, proactivity, and continuous improvement.

Core Responsibilities Include:

1. Human Resources Management

  • Implement HR policies and procedures
  • Support staff administration and workforce planning
  • Facilitate recruitment and talent development
  • Promote inclusion, diversity, and gender equality across the Bureau

2. Administrative Oversight

  • Manage office allocation and administrative workflows
  • Oversee financial budgets for the Bureau
  • Supervise and coach General Service staff
  • Conduct performance evaluations and assign priorities

3. Operational Coordination

  • Provide administrative support to all ATB sections
  • Collaborate with Deputy Directors, Chiefs, and officers
  • Liaise with other ICAO Bureaux and Regional Offices
  • Ensure smooth coordination with UN bodies and external aviation organizations

Reporting Structure

The incumbent reports directly to the Director of the Air Transport Bureau (D/ATB) and works independently to support all ATB entities. The role requires strong organizational, communication, and leadership skills to manage diverse administrative functions.

Why This Role Matters

This position is essential for ensuring that ICAO’s administrative and HR systems operate efficiently, enabling the Bureau to deliver on its global aviation mandates. The Associate Administrative Officer contributes to:

  • Strengthening organizational performance
  • Enhancing workforce capability
  • Supporting global aviation safety, security, and sustainability initiatives

Application Deadline

25 June 2026

Apply here 

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more job opportunities here

The post United Nations ICAO Associate Administrative Officer (P2) Position in Montreal: Full Role Overview, Responsibilities, and Application Details appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Empowering West Africa’s Environmental Journalists: Apply for the 2026 CJID Storytelling Fellowship
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID), in collaboration with the UNESCO Global Media Defence Fund (GMDF), has announced an exceptional opportunity for environmental journalists across West Africa. This six‑month storytelling fellowship is designed to strengthen investigative reporting on violence against environmental journalists, enhance professional capacity, and support the publication of impactful stories that […]

The post Empowering West Africa’s Environmental Journalists: Apply for the 2026 CJID Storytelling Fellowship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID), in collaboration with the UNESCO Global Media Defence Fund (GMDF), has announced an exceptional opportunity for environmental journalists across West Africa. This six‑month storytelling fellowship is designed to strengthen investigative reporting on violence against environmental journalists, enhance professional capacity, and support the publication of impactful stories that meet international standards.

Environmental journalism remains one of the most critical yet high‑risk fields in the media landscape. Reporters covering environmental degradation, illegal mining, pollution, land conflicts, and climate‑related issues often face threats, intimidation, and violence. This fellowship aims to address these challenges by equipping journalists with the tools, mentorship, and institutional support needed to document and expose attacks on environmental reporters.

About the Centre for Journalism Innovation and Development (CJID)

CJID is a leading West African media innovation and development think tank committed to advancing investigative journalism, media literacy, elections, human rights, and journalist safety. As a non‑governmental organisation, CJID works to promote democratic accountability, inclusive governance, and sustainable development across Africa. Its programs empower journalists with the skills, resources, and networks needed to produce high‑quality, impactful reporting.

About the Fellowship

The CJID–UNESCO GMDF Storytelling Fellowship is a six‑month program running from July 2026 to December 2026. It focuses on strengthening the capacity of environmental journalists to investigate and report cases of violence against environmental reporters. Fellows will receive training, mentorship, editorial support, and opportunities for publication and dissemination of their stories.

Core Objectives of the Fellowship

  1. Provide training and mentorship for journalists reporting attacks against environmental reporters.
  2. Strengthen investigative reporting capacity, enabling journalists to uncover and document cases of violence.
  3. Support the production and publication of high‑quality stories on violence against environmental journalists.
  4. Ensure dissemination of work in alignment with international reporting standards.

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: 8 June 2026
  • Fellowship Duration: July 2026 – December 2026

Applicants must ensure that all required materials are submitted before the deadline.

How to Apply

Interested candidates must complete and submit the application form by Monday, 8 June 2026. Applicants will be required to provide:

  • Full name
  • Gender
  • Contact email
  • Phone number (with country code)
  • Country of residence
  • Links to professional or social media platforms

Applicants should also ensure that all seven sections of the form are completed before submission.

Selection Process

Applications will undergo a thorough review by CJID and its partners. Shortlisted candidates will be contacted for the next stage of the selection process. Only shortlisted applicants will receive follow‑up communication.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Environmental journalists play a vital role in exposing environmental crimes, advocating for climate justice, and protecting natural resources. However, they often face threats that hinder their work. This fellowship provides:

  • A safe platform for journalists to document violence
  • Professional mentorship from experienced investigative reporters
  • Opportunities to publish stories with regional and international reach
  • Skills that strengthen long‑term career growth in environmental journalism

This initiative is a significant step toward protecting press freedom and amplifying the voices of journalists working in high‑risk environmental contexts.

 

For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities here

 

The post Empowering West Africa’s Environmental Journalists: Apply for the 2026 CJID Storytelling Fellowship appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Postdoctoral Fellowship in Cities and Communities 2026: A Major Opportunity for Global Researchers and Early-Career Scholars
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Researchers and emerging academics passionate about urban development, sustainability, social transformation, and community-focused research now have an exciting opportunity to advance their careers through the prestigious Postdoctoral Fellowship in Cities and Communities 2026. The fellowship provides a unique platform for early-career scholars to engage in high-level interdisciplinary research focused on some of the world’s most […]

The post Postdoctoral Fellowship in Cities and Communities 2026: A Major Opportunity for Global Researchers and Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers and emerging academics passionate about urban development, sustainability, social transformation, and community-focused research now have an exciting opportunity to advance their careers through the prestigious Postdoctoral Fellowship in Cities and Communities 2026.

The fellowship provides a unique platform for early-career scholars to engage in high-level interdisciplinary research focused on some of the world’s most pressing urban and societal challenges. As cities continue to expand rapidly and communities worldwide face increasing environmental, social, economic, and infrastructural pressures, the need for innovative research and evidence-based solutions has never been more important.

This fellowship is specifically designed to support outstanding postdoctoral researchers whose work contributes to understanding and improving cities, communities, urban systems, and sustainable development. The program offers selected fellows the opportunity to deepen their research, collaborate with leading academics, expand professional networks, and contribute to transformative solutions that shape the future of communities globally.

For researchers looking to build a strong academic profile while contributing to impactful and policy-relevant work, this fellowship represents a significant career development opportunity.

Why the Fellowship Matters in Today’s World

Urbanization is one of the defining global trends of the 21st century. Millions of people continue moving into urban areas, creating both opportunities and challenges for governments, institutions, policymakers, and communities.

Cities worldwide are currently facing complex issues such as:

  • Climate change and environmental sustainability
  • Housing shortages and affordability crises
  • Transportation and infrastructure challenges
  • Social inequality and exclusion
  • Public health concerns
  • Energy sustainability
  • Digital transformation and smart city integration
  • Migration and population growth
  • Disaster preparedness and resilience

At the same time, communities are increasingly seeking inclusive, innovative, and sustainable development models that improve quality of life while protecting environmental and social systems.

The Postdoctoral Fellowship in Cities and Communities seeks to support researchers capable of contributing fresh ideas, innovative methodologies, and evidence-based recommendations that can address these evolving global challenges.

What Fellows Can Expect from the Program

The fellowship provides an academically enriching and professionally rewarding environment for scholars pursuing advanced interdisciplinary research.

Selected fellows may benefit from:

  • Dedicated research time and institutional support
  • Opportunities to collaborate with leading experts and faculty members
  • Access to research resources and academic networks
  • Participation in seminars, workshops, and conferences
  • Opportunities for publication and dissemination of research findings
  • Career development and mentorship opportunities
  • Exposure to interdisciplinary research approaches

The fellowship aims to strengthen both individual academic careers and broader research ecosystems by encouraging collaboration across disciplines and sectors.

Key Research Areas and Themes

Applicants whose work aligns with urban and community-focused themes are especially encouraged to apply. Relevant research areas may include:

  1. Urban Sustainability
    Research on sustainable cities, climate resilience, green infrastructure, renewable energy integration, and environmental governance.
  2. Community Development
    Studies focused on inclusive communities, social cohesion, civic engagement, local governance, and participatory planning.
  3. Housing and Urban Planning
    Research related to housing systems, spatial planning, urban design, smart cities, mobility, and infrastructure development.
  4. Social Equity and Inclusion
    Projects addressing inequality, access to services, marginalized populations, gender inclusion, migration, and urban poverty.
  5. Public Health and Well-being
    Research examining the relationship between urban environments, health systems, mental health, and quality of life.
  6. Technology and Innovation
    Studies exploring digital transformation, data-driven governance, innovation ecosystems, and urban technologies.

The interdisciplinary nature of the fellowship makes it particularly attractive to scholars who combine perspectives from multiple academic fields.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is ideal for:

  • Recent PhD graduates
  • Early-career researchers
  • Urban studies scholars
  • Sustainability researchers
  • Social scientists
  • Geographers
  • Public policy experts
  • Environmental researchers
  • Architects and planners
  • Community development specialists

Applicants with a demonstrated commitment to producing impactful and socially relevant research are likely to stand out during the selection process.

Benefits of Participating in the Fellowship

Participating in a postdoctoral fellowship can significantly strengthen an academic or research career. Beyond research funding and institutional affiliation, fellows gain opportunities to:

  • Expand professional academic networks
  • Increase publication output
  • Collaborate internationally
  • Build interdisciplinary expertise
  • Enhance teaching and mentorship experience
  • Strengthen eligibility for future academic and research positions
  • Contribute to global policy and development conversations

For many researchers, postdoctoral fellowships also serve as a bridge toward long-term academic appointments, leadership roles, and international collaborations.

Application Process

Interested applicants are encouraged to carefully review the official fellowship requirements and prepare all necessary application materials.

Common application requirements may include:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Cover letter or statement of interest
  • Research proposal
  • Academic transcripts
  • Writing samples or publications
  • Letters of recommendation

Applicants should ensure their research proposals clearly demonstrate:

  • Relevance to cities and communities
  • Innovation and originality
  • Interdisciplinary value
  • Potential societal impact
  • Alignment with the fellowship’s objectives

Application Deadline

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and monitor the official application portal for any updates regarding deadlines, eligibility criteria, and supporting documentation requirements.

How to Apply

Applicants can submit their applications using the official portal

View complete details about this opportunity here.

click here to dicover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY

The post Postdoctoral Fellowship in Cities and Communities 2026: A Major Opportunity for Global Researchers and Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNFPA Opens International Consultancy Opportunity for Experts in Comprehensive Sexuality Education and Humanitarian Response
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) has announced an exciting international consultancy opportunity for experienced professionals passionate about humanitarian response, youth empowerment, gender equality, and Comprehensive Sexuality Education (CSE). The consultancy, titled “International Consultancy – Develop Programmatic Guidance and Essential Content for CSE in Humanitarian Settings,” offers qualified experts the opportunity to contribute to global […]

The post UNFPA Opens International Consultancy Opportunity for Experts in Comprehensive Sexuality Education and Humanitarian Response appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) has announced an exciting international consultancy opportunity for experienced professionals passionate about humanitarian response, youth empowerment, gender equality, and Comprehensive Sexuality Education (CSE). The consultancy, titled “International Consultancy – Develop Programmatic Guidance and Essential Content for CSE in Humanitarian Settings,” offers qualified experts the opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian and development efforts that directly impact adolescents and young people affected by crises.

The consultancy will support the development of evidence-based and field-ready guidance for delivering Comprehensive Sexuality Education in humanitarian settings, especially in fragile and crisis-affected regions where young people face heightened risks related to sexual and reproductive health, gender-based violence, child marriage, and lack of access to critical information and services.

This opportunity comes at a time when humanitarian crises worldwide continue to increase in scale and complexity, particularly in regions affected by conflict, displacement, climate shocks, and social instability. Adolescents and youth often make up a significant proportion of affected populations, yet access to age-appropriate sexuality education and life-saving reproductive health information remains severely limited.

UNFPA aims to bridge this gap by developing practical and operational guidance that can help humanitarian actors deliver gender-transformative, trauma-informed Comprehensive Sexuality Education in emergency contexts.

About the Consultancy Opportunity

The consultancy is being coordinated under the supervision of the UNFPA Arab States Regional Office in collaboration with the Global CSE Programme Team and other UNFPA regional and country offices.

The selected consultant will work closely with:

  • Technical Working Groups (TWGs)
  • Youth leaders
  • Humanitarian experts
  • UNFPA regional offices
  • Country offices
  • Strategic development partners

The assignment will focus on creating a Technical Guidance Document that can support country offices and humanitarian teams in implementing effective CSE programming in refugee camps, host communities, conflict zones, displacement settings, and other humanitarian environments.

The consultancy is expected to run for six months and will involve extensive collaboration, research, stakeholder consultations, validation workshops, and technical writing.

Why This Consultancy Is Important

UNFPA continues to champion global access to sexual and reproductive health rights and youth empowerment. In humanitarian settings, access to reliable sexuality education is often disrupted, leaving adolescents vulnerable to:

  • Gender-based violence
  • Early and forced marriage
  • Sexual exploitation
  • Unintended pregnancies
  • Limited healthcare access
  • Poor mental and emotional well-being

Traditional education and health systems frequently collapse during emergencies, creating an urgent need for innovative and adaptable programming approaches.

This consultancy seeks to develop practical tools and implementation models that can work even in highly unstable environments where access to schools, healthcare, and community services may be limited or interrupted.

The resulting guidance will help humanitarian actors:

  1. Deliver age-appropriate sexuality education
  2. Strengthen youth protection and empowerment
  3. Improve referrals to SRHR and GBV services
  4. Support young people’s rights, dignity, and bodily autonomy
  5. Build safer and more inclusive humanitarian responses

Key Responsibilities of the Consultant

The selected consultant will be responsible for several major deliverables throughout the consultancy period.

1. Inception Report

The consultant will prepare a detailed inception report outlining the proposed methodology, workplan, participatory processes, and collaboration strategies for the assignment.

2. Conducting a Scoping Review

The consultant will carry out a rapid review of:

  • Peer-reviewed literature
  • Humanitarian education materials
  • Existing CSE resources
  • Global best practices
  • Youth-centered approaches

The review will identify:

  • Existing gaps in humanitarian CSE programming
  • Challenges affecting vulnerable populations
  • Promising interventions and implementation models

3. Developing Programmatic Guidance

One of the most critical aspects of the consultancy is creating practical operational guidance for humanitarian teams.

The guidance document will include:

  • Strategies for implementing CSE programs in emergencies
  • Decision-making frameworks
  • Monitoring and evaluation mechanisms
  • Service referral pathways
  • Trauma-informed and gender-transformative approaches
  • Adapted lesson plans and essential educational content

4. Facilitating Collaboration and Validation Workshops

The consultant will co-facilitate validation workshops with UNFPA regional offices in:

  • Arab States
  • East and Southern Africa
  • West and Central Africa

These consultations will help ensure the final guidance is contextually relevant, evidence-based, and operationally effective.

Skills and Experience UNFPA Is Looking For

UNFPA is searching for highly experienced professionals with deep technical expertise in:

  • Humanitarian programming
  • Sexual and reproductive health rights
  • Youth engagement
  • Comprehensive Sexuality Education
  • Curriculum development
  • Policy and technical guidance writing

Candidates should also demonstrate:

  • Strong communication skills
  • Stakeholder coordination experience
  • Analytical thinking
  • Cross-cultural collaboration abilities
  • Commitment to gender equality and inclusion

Experience working with UN agencies, governments, NGOs, or international development organizations will be considered an added advantage.

Work Environment and Inclusion

UNFPA strongly promotes diversity, equity, inclusion, and gender equality within its workforce. The organization encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Indigenous populations
  • Minority groups
  • Underrepresented communities
  • LGBTQIA+ individuals

The organization remains committed to building an inclusive work environment where professionals from diverse backgrounds can contribute meaningfully to global humanitarian and development goals.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for this consultancy opportunity is 4 June 2026. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible due to the highly competitive nature of UN consultancy opportunities.

How to Apply

Applications can be submitted through the official application portal

Explore full details about the opportunity here.

Click this link to Discover more global job opportunities on OFY

The post UNFPA Opens International Consultancy Opportunity for Experts in Comprehensive Sexuality Education and Humanitarian Response appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: TWAS‑CUI Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Researchers
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The TWAS‑CUI Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme 2026 is a prestigious research opportunity designed to support outstanding young scientists from developing countries who wish to advance their expertise in natural sciences, applied sciences, and selected social sciences. Hosted by COMSATS University Islamabad (CUI) in Pakistan, this fellowship provides a structured, well‑supported environment for researchers to deepen their […]

The post TWAS‑CUI Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The TWAS‑CUI Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme 2026 is a prestigious research opportunity designed to support outstanding young scientists from developing countries who wish to advance their expertise in natural sciences, applied sciences, and selected social sciences. Hosted by COMSATS University Islamabad (CUI) in Pakistan, this fellowship provides a structured, well‑supported environment for researchers to deepen their academic contributions while collaborating with world‑class faculty.

This comprehensive guide explains every detail of the programme, including eligibility, fields of study, benefits, application requirements, deadlines, and contact information. It is optimized for high search visibility and clarity.

Programme Overview

The TWAS‑CUI Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme supports early‑career researchers from developing countries (excluding Pakistan) who aim to conduct postdoctoral research for up to six months at COMSATS University Islamabad. An additional six‑month extension may be granted, though no financial support is provided during the extended period.

Key Programme Facts

  • Host Institution: COMSATS University Islamabad (CUI), Pakistan
  • Administered by: The World Academy of Sciences (TWAS)
  • Duration: Up to 6 months (with possible 6‑month no‑cost extension)
  • Opening Date: 8 May 2026
  • Closing Date: 15 June 2026
  • Level: Postdoctoral
  • Minimum Degree Required: PhD obtained within the last 5 years
  • Language of Instruction: English
  • Eligible Applicants: Nationals of developing countries (excluding Pakistan)

Fields of Research

CUI offers a wide range of research areas across natural sciences, applied sciences, and social sciences.

Natural & Applied Sciences

  • Computer Science
  • Computer Engineering
  • Interdisciplinary Research Centre in Biomedical Materials (IRCBM)
  • Mathematics
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Biotechnology
  • Chemistry
  • Pharmacy

Social & Economic Sciences

  • Management Science
  • Development Studies

These fields align with TWAS scientific categories such as:

  • Interdisciplinary Sciences
  • Structural, Cell & Molecular Biology
  • Biological Systems & Organisms
  • Chemical Sciences
  • Engineering Sciences
  • Astronomy, Space & Earth Sciences
  • Mathematical Sciences
  • Physics
  • Social & Economic Sciences

Fellowship Benefits

CUI provides a monthly stipend intended to cover:

  • Food
  • Accommodation
  • Health insurance
  • Local living expenses

Important notes:

  • The stipend cannot be converted into foreign currency.
  • No stipend is provided during the optional extension period.

Acceptance Letter Requirement

Applicants must secure a Preliminary Acceptance Letter from a CUI supervisor.

Steps to Obtain the Acceptance Letter

  1. Review the list of available host supervisors (PDFs provided on the TWAS website).
  2. Contact a suitable supervisor for academic discussion.
  3. Formally request the acceptance letter by writing to: Mr. Kashif Masood, Deputy Registrar (International) Email: cuitwasfellowships@comsats.edu.pk

Only acceptance letters dated the same year as the application are valid.

Application Process

All applications must be submitted online through the TWAS portal.

Required Documents

  • Acceptance Letter from CUI
  • Signed reference letters on official letterhead
  • Completed online application form
  • Supporting academic documents

Important Notes

  • Applications without an acceptance letter will not be considered.
  • Reference letters must be uploaded via the online portal only.
  • All materials must be submitted before 15 June 2026.

Contact Information

TWAS Fellowships Office

ICTP Campus, Strada Costiera 11 34151 Trieste, Italy Tel: +39 040 2240-3555 Email: fellowships@twas.org

COMSATS University Islamabad (CUI)

Mr. Kashif Masood Deputy Registrar (International) Park Road, Tarlai Kalan Islamabad 45550, Pakistan Tel: (+9251) 9049 5272 Fax: (+9251) 924 7010 Email: cuitwasfellowships@comsats.edu.pk

Application Documents

  • Online application tutorial (PDF)
  • Sample preliminary acceptance letter (PDF)
  • Foreign student information sheet (DOCX)
  • Host supervisors list – Islamabad campus (PDF)
  • Host supervisors list – Lahore campus (PDF)

 

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here

Discover more fellowship opportunities here 

The post TWAS‑CUI Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for International Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: HPI Fellowships at the University of Cape Town 2026: Fully Funded PhD and Postdoctoral Research Opportunities for African Scholars
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Hasso Plattner Institute (HPI) Research School at the University of Cape Town (UCT) has opened an exciting opportunity for talented researchers across Africa through its prestigious HPI Fellowships programme. This highly competitive fellowship supports outstanding PhD and Postdoctoral candidates who are passionate about developing innovative solutions to technology and information systems challenges affecting the […]

The post HPI Fellowships at the University of Cape Town 2026: Fully Funded PhD and Postdoctoral Research Opportunities for African Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Hasso Plattner Institute (HPI) Research School at the University of Cape Town (UCT) has opened an exciting opportunity for talented researchers across Africa through its prestigious HPI Fellowships programme. This highly competitive fellowship supports outstanding PhD and Postdoctoral candidates who are passionate about developing innovative solutions to technology and information systems challenges affecting the African continent.

Hosted by one of Africa’s leading universities, the University of Cape Town, the fellowship is designed to nurture the next generation of researchers, innovators, and academic leaders in Computer Science and Information Systems. Successful candidates will join a vibrant network of scholars and researchers working on groundbreaking projects with direct relevance to African communities and global technological advancement.

About the HPI Research School at UCT

The HPI Research School at UCT is affiliated with the School of Information Technology at the University of Cape Town and receives support from the Hasso Plattner Institute for Digital Engineering. Since its establishment, the research school has built a strong reputation for advancing research that addresses real-world challenges through innovative computing and information systems solutions.

Researchers within the programme focus on technologies and systems that can improve lives across developing regions, particularly within Africa. The school brings together experts from diverse disciplines and collaborates with international technology partners, creating an environment where innovation, research excellence, and social impact intersect.

The programme particularly encourages research that addresses African challenges and contributes practical solutions in areas such as digital development, information access, communication technologies, artificial intelligence, data science, education technologies, and community-centered computing.

Fellowship Benefits

One of the strongest aspects of the HPI Fellowship is the comprehensive financial support offered to successful candidates.

Selected fellows will receive:

  • Monthly funding of approximately EUR 1,050 (paid in South African Rand up to ZAR 17,000 per month)
  • A one-time equipment allowance of EUR 1,000
  • Annual conference support of up to EUR 2,000
  • Access to world-class research facilities at the University of Cape Town
  • Mentorship from leading African and international researchers
  • Opportunities to collaborate with fellow researchers from across Africa
  • Exposure to international academic networks and conferences

The fellowship can support candidates for up to three years, subject to satisfactory academic progress and degree requirements.

Importantly, recipients are not required to provide any service in exchange for the funding, allowing them to focus entirely on their research and academic development.

Research Areas Available

Applicants are expected to propose research topics that align with the expertise of supervisors within the HPI Research School.

Research areas include:

Computer Science

  • Natural Language Processing
  • Human-Computer Interaction
  • Knowledge Engineering
  • Network Measurements
  • Performance Engineering
  • Software Defined Networking
  • Digital Libraries
  • Information Retrieval
  • Educational Technology

Information Systems

  • ICT Innovations in Organizations and Society
  • Information Systems Management
  • Enterprise Systems
  • Business Process Management
  • ICT for Development (ICT4D)
  • Information Systems Education
  • Community Information and Communication Services

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the research interests of potential supervisors before submitting their proposals.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the fellowship, applicants must meet specific academic and language requirements.

Academic Requirements

For PhD Fellowship Applicants:

  • Must possess a Master’s Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a closely related discipline.
  • Must demonstrate strong academic performance.
  • Must have completed a high-quality dissertation or thesis.

For Postdoctoral Fellowship Applicants:

  • Must possess a PhD in a relevant field.
  • Must demonstrate a strong research background and publication potential.

English Language Requirements

Applicants whose previous education was not conducted in English may be required to provide evidence of English proficiency through:

  • TOEFL score of at least 570 (paper-based) or 230 (computer-based), or
  • IELTS overall band score of 7.0 with no section below 6.0

Applicants who studied in English are generally exempt from this requirement.

Application Process

Applicants are required to prepare a detailed research proposal outlining:

  1. The research problem they intend to investigate.
  2. Research objectives and questions.
  3. Proposed methodology.
  4. Expected outcomes and impact.
  5. Alignment with the expertise of HPI supervisors.

Candidates may indicate up to three potential supervisors during the application process.

The programme strongly recommends that applicants review supervisors’ research publications and areas of specialization before submitting an application.

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews and required to arrange three academic reference letters.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Africa continues to face unique technological, social, and developmental challenges that require locally informed solutions. The HPI Fellowship creates a platform for talented researchers to contribute meaningful innovations that address these challenges while advancing their academic careers.

The programme not only offers financial support but also provides access to a powerful network of scholars, mentors, researchers, and technology leaders. Fellows gain opportunities to publish internationally, present research at global conferences, and contribute to transformative projects that have a lasting impact on communities across Africa.

For aspiring researchers looking to build a strong academic and professional future while contributing to Africa’s technological development, the HPI Fellowship at the University of Cape Town represents one of the continent’s most valuable research opportunities.

Application Deadline

Applications open annually on 15 June and close on 15 August for admission in February of the following year.

Apply here

For more information, applicants can visit the official HPI Fellowship page and review all eligibility requirements and application guidelines before submission.

Click here to discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY.

The post HPI Fellowships at the University of Cape Town 2026: Fully Funded PhD and Postdoctoral Research Opportunities for African Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Canada 2026 One-Year Postdoctoral Fellowship for Refugee Youth Research at York University and ISSofBC
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Researchers passionate about refugee education, migration, and social inclusion now have an exciting opportunity to join a major community-based research initiative in Canada through the One-Year Postdoctoral Fellow Position under the UnborderED Knowledge project. Hosted by the Immigrant Services Society of British Columbia (ISSofBC) in collaboration with York University, this fellowship is designed for scholars […]

The post Canada 2026 One-Year Postdoctoral Fellowship for Refugee Youth Research at York University and ISSofBC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers passionate about refugee education, migration, and social inclusion now have an exciting opportunity to join a major community-based research initiative in Canada through the One-Year Postdoctoral Fellow Position under the UnborderED Knowledge project.

Hosted by the Immigrant Services Society of British Columbia (ISSofBC) in collaboration with York University, this fellowship is designed for scholars interested in conducting impactful research focused on refugee youth whose educational journeys have been disrupted by displacement, conflict, and forced migration.

The position combines academic research, policy engagement, and community collaboration, making it one of the most meaningful postdoctoral opportunities currently available in the migration and refugee studies field.

About the Fellowship

The fellowship is part of the SSHRC-funded UnborderED Knowledge initiative, a project dedicated to improving educational access and opportunities for displaced populations. The successful candidate will work closely with ISSofBC’s Settlement and Refugee Services Division while collaborating with academic researchers from York University.

Based in Metro Vancouver, the fellow will contribute to research examining how refugee youth navigate post-secondary education, settlement services, employment systems, and community belonging after displacement.

The research aims to generate practical recommendations that can improve programs, institutional policies, and educational opportunities for newcomer and refugee communities across Canada.

Key Responsibilities

The selected Postdoctoral Fellow will:

  • Conduct qualitative and quantitative research activities
  • Collect and analyze data related to refugee youth experiences
  • Engage directly with refugee and newcomer communities
  • Conduct interviews, focus groups, and community consultations
  • Contribute to reports, publications, and policy briefs
  • Participate in workshops, conferences, and public engagement activities
  • Collaborate with community organizations and academic partners
  • Support knowledge mobilization and policy development initiatives

The role emphasizes community-engaged and equity-centered research approaches, ensuring that research outcomes remain relevant and beneficial to affected communities.

Who Should Apply?

The fellowship is ideal for:

  • Scholars with lived experience of forced migration
  • Researchers in migration studies and refugee education
  • Social science and public policy researchers
  • Community development specialists
  • Education and settlement researchers
  • Academics interested in social inclusion and newcomer integration

Applicants with experience working alongside refugee or displaced communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

Qualifications

To qualify, applicants should possess:

  • A graduate degree relevant to the fellowship area
  • Strong academic and research experience
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills
  • Experience conducting community-based or participatory research
  • Knowledge of migration, refugee protection, settlement, or education issues

Research publication experience and knowledge mobilization skills are considered added advantages.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Unlike many traditional academic fellowships, this position offers researchers the chance to directly contribute to social impact and policy change. The selected fellow will work within one of Canada’s leading immigrant-serving organizations while gaining valuable experience in applied research, stakeholder engagement, and community collaboration.

The fellowship also provides opportunities for professional growth, networking, academic publishing, and participation in national conversations around refugee education and inclusion.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the One-Year Postdoctoral Fellow Position is:

12 June 2026

Interested applicants should submit a current CV and a brief cover letter explaining their interest and suitability for the position.

How to Apply

Applicants can learn more and access the official opportunity page here:

Applications should be submitted via email:jobs@issbc.org

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post Canada 2026 One-Year Postdoctoral Fellowship for Refugee Youth Research at York University and ISSofBC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LSE Fellow in Health Policy 2026: London School of Economics Opens Prestigious Academic Job Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE), one of the world’s leading social science institutions, is inviting applications for the position of LSE Fellow in Health Policy. This exciting academic opportunity offers early-career researchers and scholars the chance to join a globally respected institution while contributing to teaching and research within the field […]

The post LSE Fellow in Health Policy 2026: London School of Economics Opens Prestigious Academic Job Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE), one of the world’s leading social science institutions, is inviting applications for the position of LSE Fellow in Health Policy. This exciting academic opportunity offers early-career researchers and scholars the chance to join a globally respected institution while contributing to teaching and research within the field of health policy.

Based in London, United Kingdom, the fellowship position is hosted within LSE Health, a globally recognized center for research and policy engagement focusing on healthcare systems, public health, social care, and health economics. The role presents a unique opportunity for academics interested in shaping discussions around healthcare policy, inequality, public health systems, and evidence-based policymaking at both national and international levels.

About the LSE Fellow in Health Policy Position

The LSE Fellow role is designed for scholars who are passionate about academic teaching, impactful research, and policy engagement. The successful candidate will contribute to LSE’s strong tradition of interdisciplinary research and teaching excellence while working alongside internationally respected academics and policy experts.

The fellow will participate in both undergraduate and postgraduate teaching activities and support the department’s ongoing research agenda in health policy and related areas.

The role offers exposure to one of the most intellectually vibrant academic environments in the world, with opportunities to collaborate across disciplines including:

  • Public Health
  • Health Economics
  • Social Policy
  • Healthcare Management
  • Global Health
  • Political Science
  • Sociology
  • Public Administration

Key Responsibilities

The selected LSE Fellow in Health Policy will undertake a combination of teaching, research, and academic service responsibilities.

Core duties include:

Teaching Responsibilities

  • Deliver high-quality undergraduate and postgraduate teaching
  • Conduct seminars, lectures, and tutorials
  • Provide academic guidance and supervision to students
  • Participate in curriculum development and course delivery
  • Contribute to assessment and examination processes

Research Responsibilities

  • Conduct independent and collaborative research in health policy or related areas
  • Publish high-quality academic research
  • Contribute to ongoing departmental research projects
  • Participate in academic conferences and research workshops
  • Engage in policy-oriented research discussions

Academic Contribution

  • Participate in departmental meetings and academic activities
  • Support the intellectual life of the department
  • Contribute to outreach and engagement initiatives
  • Collaborate with colleagues across LSE Health and related departments

Why This Opportunity Stands Out

The London School of Economics is consistently ranked among the world’s leading universities for social sciences and policy studies. Securing an academic position at LSE provides scholars with exceptional professional visibility and access to influential global academic networks.

The fellowship offers several major advantages, including:

  • Opportunity to teach at a world-renowned university
  • Access to leading researchers and policymakers
  • Exposure to international health policy debates
  • Strong academic networking opportunities
  • Research collaboration prospects
  • Career development within a globally respected institution
  • Experience working in an interdisciplinary academic environment

The position is particularly suitable for emerging academics seeking to build long-term careers in research, higher education, and policy engagement.

Qualifications and Eligibility

Applicants are expected to possess:

  • A completed PhD or be close to completion in Health Policy, Public Health, Health Economics, Social Policy, or a related discipline
  • Strong evidence of research potential
  • Teaching experience or the ability to contribute effectively to university teaching
  • Excellent communication and academic writing skills
  • Demonstrated interest in health policy research and analysis

Candidates with publication records, interdisciplinary research interests, or policy engagement experience may have an added advantage during the selection process.

Application Deadline

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply before the official deadline stated on the LSE vacancies portal.

How to Apply

Applicants can access the official application page and full job details here:

Prospective candidates should carefully review all application instructions, eligibility requirements, and supporting document guidelines before submitting their applications.

For scholars interested in advancing their academic careers within one of the world’s top institutions for social science research and policy engagement, the LSE Fellow in Health Policy position represents an outstanding international opportunity.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

The post LSE Fellow in Health Policy 2026: London School of Economics Opens Prestigious Academic Job Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
